line: GROVE_RESULT: implemented the F15 first slice for governed fork capture, branch metadata, and isolation semantics across core, CLI, and daemon surfaces.

event.summary: implemented the F15 first slice for governed fork capture, branch metadata, and isolation semantics across core, CLI, and daemon surfaces.

items: `crates/membrain-core/src/api.rs`
`crates/membrain-core/src/brain_store.rs`
`crates/membrain-core/src/engine/mod.rs`
`crates/membrain-core/src/engine/fork.rs`
`crates/membrain-core/src/lib.rs`
`crates/membrain-daemon/src/rpc.rs`
`crates/membrain-daemon/src/daemon.rs`
`crates/membrain-cli/src/main.rs`

line: - Implemented the bead-scoped F15 slice as governed namespace metadata, not unrestricted cloning.

items: Implemented the bead-scoped F15 slice as governed namespace metadata, not unrestricted cloning.

line: - Used inheritance-by-reference metadata with explicit `ForkInheritance` (`public|shared|all`) and stable fork lifecycle state.

items: Used inheritance-by-reference metadata with explicit `ForkInheritance` (`public|shared|all`) and stable fork lifecycle state.

line: - Added minimal `merge_fork` behavior sufficient for slice completeness and interface parity, but kept it metadata/audit oriented rather than full belief-resolution logic.

items: Added minimal `merge_fork` behavior sufficient for slice completeness and interface parity, but kept it metadata/audit oriented rather than full belief-resolution logic.

line: - Kept the new state in `BrainStore` as an in-memory fork registry to match the repo’s current in-memory snapshot/working-state patterns, while preserving compatibility with the already-defined durable schema constants.

items: Kept the new state in `BrainStore` as an in-memory fork registry to match the repo’s current in-memory snapshot/working-state patterns, while preserving compatibility with the already-defined durable schema constants.

line: - Wired both CLI and daemon/RPC to the same core fork/merge vocabulary.

items: Wired both CLI and daemon/RPC to the same core fork/merge vocabulary.

line: - The repo already had F15 schema/docs/MCP contract scaffolding, but no core implementation path.

items: The repo already had F15 schema/docs/MCP contract scaffolding, but no core implementation path.

line: - Narrow validation works better here than broad buckets because unrelated existing failures still exist in `membrain-core` tests.

items: Narrow validation works better here than broad buckets because unrelated existing failures still exist in `membrain-core` tests.

line: - Wrapper crates needed `ForkConfig`/`MergeConfig` imported from `membrain_core::brain_store`, not `membrain_core::api`.

items: Wrapper crates needed `ForkConfig`/`MergeConfig` imported from `membrain_core::brain_store`, not `membrain_core::api`.

line: - `merge_fork` is intentionally only the first safe layer for this bead: counts/conflict reporting are metadata-driven and not yet backed by full authoritative memory harvest + belief-version reconciliation.

items: `merge_fork` is intentionally only the first safe layer for this bead: counts/conflict reporting are metadata-driven and not yet backed by full authoritative memory harvest + belief-version reconciliation.

line: - Durable SQLite persistence for `brain_forks` / `fork_merge_log` is still schema-only; this slice stores fork state in the core facade for now.

items: Durable SQLite persistence for `brain_forks` / `fork_merge_log` is still schema-only; this slice stores fork state in the core facade for now.

line: - `cargo check -p membrain-core -p membrain-cli -p membrain-daemon` passes, but `membrain-core`’s broader unit bucket still contains unrelated pre-existing failures outside this slice.

items: `cargo check -p membrain-core -p membrain-cli -p membrain-daemon` passes, but `membrain-core`’s broader unit bucket still contains unrelated pre-existing failures outside this slice.
